data.description:
## Summary

`rebar transition <parent> closed --force` currently SKIPS the unresolved-children guard, letting a parent (epic/story) close while it has open (non-closed) children. The child-closure relationship is a STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY invariant (a parent is not complete if its children are not), NOT a quality gate — so --force should never be able to violate it. (Contrast: --force-close legitimately bypasses the signature/completion-verifier QUALITY gate for emergencies; --force legitimately bypasses the plan-review gate. Neither should bypass child-closure.)

Surfaced live: an epic was force-closed over 2 open children (then reopened + properly resolved by detaching the follow-ups). The footgun should not exist.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create an epic with an open child (e.g. `rebar create epic E`, `rebar create task T --parent E`).
2. `rebar transition E in_progress closed --force` (or `--force --force-close=...`).
3. Observe: the epic closes with a WARNING but succeeds, leaving an open child under a closed parent.

## Expected vs Actual

EXPECTED: closing a ticket with any open (non-closed) child is ALWAYS refused, regardless of --force / --force-close. The only ways to close are: close/resolve the children first, or detach them (re-home). The error should NOT offer --force as an escape for the children guard.

ACTUAL: --force skips the unresolved-children guard (transition.py:~47-48 help + ~416-425 logic + the force branch at ~119), closing the parent with children unresolved (only a Warning).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The unresolved-children close guard is enforced UNCONDITIONALLY: --force and --force-close do NOT bypass it; closing a ticket with >=1 open child always errors.
- [ ] --force still bypasses the plan-review gate; --force-close still bypasses the signature/completion-verifier requirement (those are unchanged).
- [ ] The error message no longer suggests --force as a way to close over open children; it directs to close/resolve or detach the children.
- [ ] The --force help text is updated to drop the children-guard-skip claim.
- [ ] A test asserts a parent with an open child cannot be closed even with --force/--force-close; tests covering the old force-skip behavior are updated/removed.
- [ ] make lint + full suite pass.
data.title: --force must NOT bypass the unresolved-children close guard (structural invariant, not a quality gate)
